questions.question: Explain Big O notation. What is the time complexity of binary search?
questions.answer: Big O describes worst-case time/space complexity as input grows. Binary search: O(log n) - divides search space in half each step. O(1) is constant, O(n) linear, O(n²) quadratic.
questions.explanation: Binary search works on sorted arrays by comparing the target with the middle element and discarding half the remaining elements each iteration - hence O(log n).

questions.question: What is dynamic programming? Give an example.
questions.answer: DP solves problems by breaking them into overlapping subproblems, storing solutions to avoid recomputation. Example: Fibonacci - F(n) = F(n-1) + F(n-2). Memoization (top-down) or tabulation (bottom-up).
questions.explanation: Two conditions needed for DP: optimal substructure (optimal solution contains optimal sub-solutions) and overlapping subproblems (same subproblems solved multiple times).

questions.question: Explain the difference between BFS and DFS. When would you use each?
questions.answer: BFS uses a queue, explores level by level - ideal for shortest path in unweighted graphs. DFS uses a stack (or recursion), explores depth first - ideal for cycle detection, topological sort, connected components.
questions.explanation: BFS guarantees shortest path in unweighted graphs. DFS uses less memory for deep graphs. Both are O(V+E) for time and space complexity.
